avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Windslab will continue to develop through the period. Significant areas of unstable windslab will be present mainly on North-West through North to South-East aspects above 700 metres. There will be a period of reduced stability around dawn when avalanches are likely. Cornices will be fragile and prone to collapse. Windward aspects will generally be scoured and icy with good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Southerly winds will increase to gale force for a time overnight before easing slightly and veering to the South-West during the day. Showers will mostly fall as snow above 500 metres but with rain up to 900 metres for a time around dawn. The freezing level will mostly be around 500 metres rising to 1050 metres for a time around dawn.
Observed Weather Influences: There were snow showers and bright periods with a fresh West-South-Westerly wind. The freezing level was around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of poorly bonded windslab are present in wind sheltered locations with deepest accumulations on North through East to South-East aspects above 700 metres. Fragile cornices are present above these aspects. Windward aspects are generally scoured, icy and stable. Evidence of a recent slab avalanche noted on an East aspect at 1200 metres. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snowfall and drifting will form fresh unstable windslab in wind sheltered locations on North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops and sharply defined corrie rims will be most affected. Moderately bonded accumulations will be present just below this altitude. Fragile cornices will be present. Wind exposed locations will be scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall, arriving overnight, above 600 metres initially, will be heaviest after daybreak until mid-morning above 850 metres. The winds will be mainly strong South-South-Westerly, later veering West-South-Westerly. 600 metre freezing level overnight, rising briefly to 850 metres during the morning, lowering back down to 600 metres by the end of the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: After a bright start, cloud quickly built up with light snow showers above 750 metres by early afternoon. Winds were fresh Westerly, easing as they backed to become moderate South-Westerly. The freezing level rose to around 800 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab is present in wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects above 900 metres with gully tops and steeper corrie rims most affected. Moderately bonded snow is present just below this altitude and occasionally on isolated North-West aspects above 900 metres. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and firm. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Overnight, unstable windslab will continue to form at increasingly higher elevations in steep wind sheltered locations mainly on North to East aspects. Other aspects affected through crossloading. A brief period of thawing around dawn will have limited change to stability, though cornices will be prone to collapse. Through the day, fresh unstable windslab will form on similar aspects above 850 metres. Wind exposed areas will be icy with good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Overnight showers will fall as snow at increasingly higher elevations as the freezing level briefly rises to around 1000 metres. South-Westerly winds becoming strong to gale force. Winds ease becoming fresh to strong through the day with snow showers continuing to fall above around 500 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: A bright morning with showers, heavier and more prolonged in the afternoon, falling as snow above 650 metres. Light to moderate South-Westerly winds, stronger gusts in the showers.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh accumulations of unstable windslab is present in wind sheltered locations mainly on North to East aspects above 850 metres.  Other aspects are affected through cross loading. These deposits are not yet extensive but where they gain depth are poorly bonded. Cornices have reformed on these aspects also. Areas exposed to the wind remain hard and icy with potential for serious runouts.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will continue to develop becoming more widespread and deeper on Northerly through East to South-Easterly aspects above 800 metres. Coire rims, headwalls and gullies especially affected. Large weak cornices will continue to build above same aspects threatening collapse on to slopes below. Below 850m, although deep and extensive in places the snowpack will remain generally consolidated and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds fresh to gale force South-Westerly overnight then very strong West-South-Westerly during the day. Freezing level 700m overnight before rising to summit levels for a time at daybreak then lowering to 600m by late afternoon. Precipitation falling as snow above 600m then rain and sleet to 900m for a time early morning before persistent snow fall gradually above 400m.
Observed Weather Influences: Snow showers, heavy and persistent at times during the morning above 500m, occasional light snow showers during the afternoon. Winds strong to moderate strength West-South-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab has developed and is widespread and deep on Northerly through East to South-Easterly aspects above 850 metres. Coire rims, headwalls and gullies especially affected. Large cornices have also developed above same aspects threatening collapse. Below this level some consolidation has taken place and although deep and widespread it is generally moist and stable. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised areas of windslab will continue to develop with a brief period of instability in the warming around dawn. Steep wind sheltered areas such as gullies and coire rims on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres most affected. Elsewhere there will be a shallow covering of soft stable snow. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers throughout the period will initially be snow above around 500 metres turning to rain at most levels for a brief period around dawn before turning back to snow at lower levels. Moderate South-South-Westerly winds overnight veering South-Westerly and increasing to strong during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Showers became more frequent in the afternoon and fell as snow above around 200 metres. Moderate South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was around 750 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab exist in some steep wind sheltered locations. North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres are most affected. Elsewhere there is a thin covering of stable snow.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: From the middle of the night onwards, new unstable windslab will build on North to East aspects, cross loading will affect North-West and South-East aspects. The greatest instability will be above 950 metres in steep wind sheltered areas. Cornices will be fragile and potentially trigger slopes below.  Below 950 metres localised windslab will develop on steep banks and burn lines. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm wind scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snow fall is expected from the middle of the night onwards. Strong South-West winds will increase gale to storm force, become Southerly then veer West-South-Westerly for the duration of the day. The 700 metre freezing level will briefly rise to 1000 metres during the early morning before steadily falling to 600 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Occasional snow showers on strong Westerly winds. The freezing level was around 800 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Accumulations of unstable windslab are present on Northerly to Easterly aspects above 950 metres.  The greatest instability is in steep wind sheltered terrain, such as coire rims, head walls gullies and convexities. Avalanche debris was observed on a North-East aspect at 1100 metres. Recently formed cornices are fragile. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
